title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is less about new model launches and more about the machinery around trustworthy deployment: secure agent operation, privacy controls, source-verification, and better ways to audit safety when benchmarks are missing. The strongest updates also point to a maturing ecosystem where external reviewers, policy groups, and researchers are probing how claims about risk, citations, and safeguards can actually be validated.

items.title: Cited but Not Verified examines source attribution in deep research agents
items.why_it_matters: Reliable citation checking is central to trust in agent-generated research and evidence-backed outputs.

items.title: When No Benchmark Exists proposes comparative LLM safety scoring without ground-truth labels
items.why_it_matters: It addresses how to audit model safety in real deployments before formal benchmarks exist.

items.title: Running Codex safely at OpenAI details sandboxing, approvals, and telemetry for coding agents
items.why_it_matters: It shows concrete operational controls for reducing agent misuse and containing code-execution risk.

items.title: METR reviews Anthropic’s automated R&D risk analysis
items.why_it_matters: Independent review of frontier-risk claims strengthens scrutiny around preparedness and capability-risk assessments.

items.title: OpenAI expands Trusted Access for Cyber with GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5-Cyber
items.why_it_matters: Controlled access programs shape how high-risk cyber capabilities are governed and limited to defenders.

items.title: Partnership on AI pushes AI-generated media transparency in the EU Code of Practice
items.why_it_matters: It bears on disclosure standards and governance for synthetic media in a major regulatory market.

items.title: OpenAI introduces Trusted Contact in ChatGPT for serious self-harm concerns
items.why_it_matters: This adds a new intervention mechanism for high-severity mental health safety scenarios.

items.title: How ChatGPT learns about the world while protecting privacy outlines data minimization and user controls
items.why_it_matters: Privacy safeguards and training-data controls directly affect deployment trust and regulatory compliance.

items.title: How Many Iterations to Jailbreak studies dynamic budget allocation for multi-turn LLM evaluation
items.why_it_matters: More efficient jailbreak testing could improve red-teaming coverage under limited evaluation budgets.

items.title: SoftSAE proposes adaptive sparse autoencoders for interpretability
items.why_it_matters: Better sparse feature extraction may improve mechanistic interpretability of model internals.

items.title: Crafting Reversible SFT Behaviors explores reversible fine-tuned behaviors in language models
items.why_it_matters: Understanding whether added behaviors can be isolated or reversed matters for controllability and audits.

items.title: Algospeak, Hiding in the Open analyzes the trade-off between legibility and detection avoidance
items.why_it_matters: It informs moderation systems facing adaptive evasion by users and models.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
